# Events

Source: `lib/protocols/proto/v1/events.proto`

## Messages

### Event

Event is one row of the rimsky_events log with a typed payload. Consumers
who want type-checked event streams may use this shape; rimsky's own
internals use map[string]any + JSONB and do not marshal through this proto.

See spec §9.8 of docs/history/2026-04-25-stores-redesign-design.md for the
full event-kind catalogue. When new kinds are added to the log, they should
be declared here.

| Field | Type | # | Description |
|-------|------|---|-------------|
| `id` | `int64` | 1 |  |
| `instance_id` | `string` | 2 |  |
| `node_id` | `string` | 3 |  |
| `occurred_at` | `Timestamp` | 4 |  |
| `kind` | `string` | 5 |  |
| `message_emitted` | `MessageEmittedPayload` | 10 |  |
| `message_received` | `MessageReceivedPayload` | 11 |  |
| `state_transition` | `StateTransitionPayload` | 12 |  |
| `error` | `ErrorPayload` | 13 |  |
| `work_started` | `WorkStartedPayload` | 14 |  |
| `work_completed` | `WorkCompletedPayload` | 15 |  |
| `no_op_commit` | `NoOpCommitPayload` | 17 |  |
| `heartbeat_lost` | `HeartbeatLostPayload` | 19 |  |
| `operator_override` | `OperatorOverridePayload` | 20 |  |
| `orphaned_claim_released` | `OrphanedClaimReleasedPayload` | 21 |  |
| `orphaned_claim_lost_race` | `OrphanedClaimLostRacePayload` | 22 |  |
| `work_rejected` | `WorkRejectedPayload` | 23 |  |
| `unresolved_executor` | `UnresolvedExecutorPayload` | 25 |  |
| `lock_acquired` | `LockAcquiredPayload` | 40 | New event kinds added by the stores-redesign (spec §9.8). Numbers start at 40 to skip past the existing top-level `payload_raw = 30`. Field numbers 28 and 29 are held via the `reserved 28, 29;` line above for future event-kind slots. |
| `lock_released` | `LockReleasedPayload` | 41 |  |
| `lock_orphan_reaped` | `LockOrphanReapedPayload` | 42 |  |
| `attributes_substituted` | `AttributesSubstitutedPayload` | 43 |  |
| `attributes_committed` | `AttributesCommittedPayload` | 44 |  |
| `attributes_validation_failed` | `AttributesValidationFailedPayload` | 45 |  |
| `claim_acquired` | `ClaimAcquiredPayload` | 46 |  |
| `claim_held` | `ClaimHeldPayload` | 47 |  |
| `claim_resolved` | `ClaimResolvedPayload` | 48 |  |
| `template_resolution_failed` | `TemplateResolutionFailedPayload` | 49 |  |
| `payload_raw` | `Struct` | 30 | For event kinds not in the oneof above, the raw JSON payload is surfaced here. Kept populated for all events as a convenience so consumers can fall back to untyped reading. |

### LockAcquiredPayload

LockAcquiredPayload is emitted when a supervisor successfully acquires a
lock-holder row (§13.3). `lock_kind` is one of "named" | "scope" | "claim"
and selects which of the kind-specific fields are populated.

| Field | Type | # | Description |
|-------|------|---|-------------|
| `lock_kind` | `string` | 1 |  |
| `lock_name` | `string` | 2 |  |
| `producer_name` | `string` | 3 |  |
| `scope_data` | `Struct` | 4 |  |
| `claim_id` | `string` | 5 |  |
| `supervisor_id` | `string` | 6 |  |
| `holder_id` | `string` | 7 |  |
| `resumed` | `bool` | 8 |  |

### LockReleasedPayload

LockReleasedPayload is emitted when a supervisor releases a lock-holder
row at terminal (§13.6). `action` records the release intent.

| Field | Type | # | Description |
|-------|------|---|-------------|
| `lock_kind` | `string` | 1 |  |
| `lock_name` | `string` | 2 |  |
| `producer_name` | `string` | 3 |  |
| `claim_id` | `string` | 4 |  |
| `holder_id` | `string` | 5 |  |
| `supervisor_id` | `string` | 6 |  |
| `action` | `string` | 7 |  |

### LockOrphanReapedPayload

LockOrphanReapedPayload is emitted by the scheduler's lock-holder sweep
(§13.5 step 2) when a lock-holder row's `expires_at` has passed.

| Field | Type | # | Description |
|-------|------|---|-------------|
| `lock_kind` | `string` | 1 |  |
| `lock_name` | `string` | 2 |  |
| `producer_name` | `string` | 3 |  |
| `claim_id` | `string` | 4 |  |
| `holder_id` | `string` | 5 |  |
| `prior_supervisor_id` | `string` | 6 |  |
| `expired_at` | `Timestamp` | 7 |  |

### AttributesSubstitutedPayload

AttributesSubstitutedPayload is emitted at dispatch when rimsky completes
the source-directive substitution pass that populates
`rimsky_node_attributes.data` (§5.7 / §10.2).

| Field | Type | # | Description |
|-------|------|---|-------------|
| `substituted_fields` | `repeated string` | 1 | Names of attribute schema properties that were populated by source directives during this dispatch substitution pass. |
| `omitted_fields` | `repeated string` | 2 | Names of optional attribute schema properties whose source directives failed to resolve and were therefore omitted from `data` (per §10.3). |

### AttributesCommittedPayload

AttributesCommittedPayload is emitted when an executor's terminal
`attributes_delta` (or accumulated incremental writeback) is merged into
`rimsky_node_attributes.data` and validated successfully (§5.7).

| Field | Type | # | Description |
|-------|------|---|-------------|
| `changed` | `bool` | 1 | Producer-declared change flag — drives cascade propagation (§4.3 of the node-graph design doc). Trust + audit log; rimsky does not hash-and-check. |
| `updated_fields` | `repeated string` | 2 | Names of attribute schema properties touched by this commit (executor writeback merged into `data`). |
| `change_summary` | `string` | 3 |  |

### AttributesValidationFailedPayload

AttributesValidationFailedPayload is emitted when JSON-schema validation
of `rimsky_node_attributes.data` fails at commit (§5.7.1: commit-time
`attributes_schema_failed`). Dispatch-time required-source resolution
failures are emitted as `template_resolution_failed` with their own
payload — they do not surface here.

| Field | Type | # | Description |
|-------|------|---|-------------|
| `errors` | `repeated Struct` | 2 | Structured per-error details (path, keyword, message). One Struct per schema violation. |

### ClaimAcquiredPayload

ClaimAcquiredPayload is emitted at the same moment as `lock_acquired` for
a claim-kind lock — it carries the claim-specific bookkeeping the lock
event doesn't (`hold`, on-commit/on-give-up declarations).

| Field | Type | # | Description |
|-------|------|---|-------------|
| `claim_id` | `string` | 1 |  |
| `producer_name` | `string` | 2 |  |
| `hold` | `bool` | 3 |  |
| `on_commit` | `string` | 4 |  |
| `on_give_up` | `string` | 5 |  |

### ClaimHeldPayload

ClaimHeldPayload is emitted at commit of a claiming-source node when
`hold: true` and `rimsky_claim_holders` rows are inserted, one per
terminal-leaf identified by the §11.4 DAG walk.

| Field | Type | # | Description |
|-------|------|---|-------------|
| `claim_id` | `string` | 1 |  |
| `producer_name` | `string` | 2 |  |
| `terminal_node_ids` | `repeated string` | 3 | Terminal-leaf node ids that participate in the held claim (one row per id was inserted into rimsky_claim_holders). |

### ClaimResolvedPayload

ClaimResolvedPayload is emitted when the §5.6.4 resolution algorithm
runs for a held claim — either at terminal-node release or via the
scheduler's claim-holder GC sweep (§13.5 step 3). Carries `action`,
`claim_id`, `producer_name` per spec §9.8.

| Field | Type | # | Description |
|-------|------|---|-------------|
| `action` | `string` | 1 |  |
| `claim_id` | `string` | 2 |  |
| `producer_name` | `string` | 3 |  |

### TemplateResolutionFailedPayload

TemplateResolutionFailedPayload is emitted when a required source directive
fails to resolve at dispatch (§5.7.1, §10.3, §10.4). The error class is
`template_resolution_failed`; this payload carries the directive text and
failure reason for debugging.

| Field | Type | # | Description |
|-------|------|---|-------------|
| `directive` | `string` | 1 | The unresolved directive (e.g. `{{deps.upstream.field}}`). |
| `site` | `string` | 2 | Where the directive appeared: "attribute" \| "lock_name" \| "scope". |
| `field` | `string` | 3 | For site="attribute": the attribute schema property name. |
| `reason` | `string` | 4 |  |
